news.title: Gulshan Group to invest ₹1,000 crore in Wave City housing project
news.para: Gulshan Group has launched Gulshan Empire, a premium residential project at Wave City, with an estimated investment of ₹1,000 crore. The 5.5-acre project is expected to generate sales realisation of over ₹1,200 crore.

news.title: Nagpur: Kanhan Metro may redraw skyline, TOD to unlock development along belt
news.para: The Kanhan Metro extension will enable denser construction and commercial development.  Transit-Oriented Development will concentrate growth around the new Metro stations.  Authorities may permit additional floor space index after collecting a premium.  Revenue generation will support loan repayment and future infrastructure works.  This extension aims for completion by December 2027.

news.title: FIR against Ansal Group, four others for ₹43 lakh plot fraud
news.para: Police filed a case against Ansal Group and four individuals for alleged fraud. Two buyers claim they were cheated out of Rs 43 lakh for residential plots. The buyers purchased plots from Dr Narayan Prasad and his wife Renu. They later discovered issues selling the plots without Ansal's NOC. The complainants accused the Ansal owners of conspiracy and threats.

news.title: Bihar RERA simplifies project registration process to boost ease of doing business
news.para: Bihar RERA has simplified real estate project registration procedures. A new filter-based mechanism guides applicants through each filing stage. Documentation requirements have been streamlined, reducing mandatory submissions for land details. Companies less than three years old can submit director income tax returns instead of audited financials. These reforms aim for faster, transparent, and user-friendly project approvals.

news.title: Telangana RERA orders Aparna Constructions to hand over flat pending civil suit in Hyderabad
news.para: TG RERA ordered Aparna Constructions to give a flat to a new buyer.  A registered sale deed holds validity until a court sets it aside.  The original buyers transferred rights via a power of attorney to another party.  The developer cited a title dispute and a required no objection certificate.  The authority recognized the complainant as an allottee for this specific complaint.

news.title: NCLT approves Aspect JV's resolution plan for Radius & Deserve Land Developers
news.para: The NCLT approved Aspect Group's resolution plan for Radius & Deserve Land Developers. This approval concludes the company's corporate insolvency resolution process initiated in May 2023. Bharadvaja Buildcon LLP will now take over the bankrupt real estate firm. The secured creditor will receive constructed free-sale area in a slum rehabilitation project. Construction is expected to commence within twenty-five months of plan approval.

news.title: DDA housing scheme offers 1,200 flats in Delhi's Narela at 25% discount
news.para: The Delhi Development Authority has launched a new housing scheme for government employees. This scheme offers over twelve hundred ready-to-move flats at a twenty-five percent discount. Registration for the DDA Karmajeevi Awaas Yojana 2026 begins on July twenty-fourth. Booking of these attractive Narela flats will commence on August fifteenth. Flats are available on a freehold basis, ensuring complete ownership rights for buyers.

news.title: Manglam Group to invest ₹1,000 crore in commercial project on Jaipur-Ajmer Highway
news.para: Manglam Group has launched PinkWest, a 13 lakh sq ft integrated commercial project on Jaipur-Ajmer Highway. The project is expected to be completed by 2029 and has revenue potential of about ₹1,500 crore.

news.title: 315Work Avenue leases 45,000 sq ft in Bengaluru; Welspun lease out 15,232 sq ft to Novel Jewels
news.para: 315Work Avenue leased forty-five thousand square feet of managed office space in Bengaluru. This deal was facilitated by Colliers India, a real estate consulting firm. World Trade Center Thane also signed Novel Jewels for fifteen thousand square feet. The space will support vault-enabled storage and distribution operations for the company. These transactions highlight ongoing activity in India's commercial real estate sector.

news.title: Smartworks Coworking Spaces reports ₹13.14 crore profit in Q1 FY27
news.para: Smartworks Coworking Spaces' consolidated total income increased 44.3 per cent to ₹559.66 crore during the quarter. Its operational portfolio expanded to 10.4 million sq. ft. across 54 centres.

news.title: Registration Seva Kendras to simplify property deals: Andhra Pradesh revenue minister
news.para: Revenue Minister Anagani Satya Prasad defended the new Registration Seva Kendras initiative.  He stated these centers will improve property registration transparency and citizen convenience.  The minister dismissed opposition criticism as politically motivated false propaganda.  He also pledged thorough investigations into past land encroachment allegations.  The government aims to restore accountability and protect property rights across the state.

news.title: Retail, cafes & co-working spaces planned at revamped railway stations in Bengaluru
news.para: South Western Railway is seeking consultants for commercial use of new station spaces.  These spaces at KSR Bengaluru and Yeshwantpur will host retail and food outlets.  Office and co-working spaces are also being considered for these railway assets.  Nineteen other stations across the Bengaluru division will also see commercial development.  Investors will be onboarded through auctions after consultant evaluation of proposals.

news.title: Bengaluru development body housing faces heat over fire safety violations
news.para: Many Bangalore Development Authority housing projects lack essential fire safety clearances. Residents in several complexes face risks from incomplete safety systems and missing approvals. The BDA Indraprastha complex has not received a fire no-objection certificate due to access issues. Other projects like Gunjur and Thippasandra also await their fire NOCs. The BDA is reportedly working to address these compliance and safety concerns.

news.title: Pune cantonment board directs redevelopment as 100 buildings deemed unsafe
news.para: Around 100 decades-old buildings in Pune's Camp area are structurally unsafe.  The Pune Cantonment Board has directed owners to submit redevelopment plans for these properties.  Weakened structures pose a threat to occupants and pedestrians, especially during monsoon.  Disputes between landlords and tenants have delayed redevelopment efforts significantly.  Urgent redevelopment or repairs are necessary to eliminate the risk posed by these old houses.

news.title: Projects worth ₹31,000 crore approved under Urban Challenge Fund: MoHUA official
news.para: Projects worth ₹31,000 crore have been approved under the Urban Challenge Fund within 20-25 days of its guidelines being finalised, said D Thara, additional secretary, Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs. NBFCs account for 44% of the financing for approved projects.

news.title: John Abraham buys 100-year-old Bandra bungalow for ₹84 crore; ‘no redevelopment plans’
news.para: Actor John Abraham recently bought a historic bungalow in Bandra West for eighty-four crore rupees. This property, built in nineteen twenty-six, was owned by the DeVitre family. Abraham also purchased a Linking Road bungalow in Khar for seventy-five crore rupees last December. Many Bollywood personalities invest heavily in Mumbai real estate for personal and investment reasons. Celebrities often prefer properties in the Bandra to Andheri suburbs.

news.title: Redevelopment agreement not a property receipt, so not taxable: ITAT
news.para: Mumbai ITAT ruled agreement registration does not mean property receipt.  Tax cannot be levied on future property rights under anti-abuse provisions.  The tribunal allowed a taxpayer's appeal against a ₹1.38 crore tax addition.  Registration creates contractual rights, not actual property possession or receipt.  This decision clarifies tax treatment for ongoing redevelopment projects in Mumbai.

news.title: Gurugram civic body faces property ID backlog, over 5,000 applications yet to be processed
news.para: Gurugram's civic body faces thousands of pending property ID objections and applications.  Many cases remain with the MCG, while others are returned to residents for documents.  Fresh property ID applications also show significant delays at the civic body's level.  Recent staff transfers followed corruption allegations and file tampering concerns.  This situation prolongs the process for property owners seeking record corrections.

news.title: Haryana government restores ban on stilt-plus-four building approvals
news.para: Haryana's town planning department has stopped new stilt-plus-four building approvals statewide. This decision follows proceedings before the Punjab and Haryana High Court on Wednesday. The department cited an unfinished framework for online building plan approvals and administrative mechanisms. All pending and fresh applications for these buildings will not be approved. The statewide freeze remains until a regulatory framework is established or court orders change.

news.title: KFin Technologies leases over 2.42 lakh sq ft office in Hyderabad’s Kokapet
news.para: KFin Technologies has leased over 2.42 lakh sq ft of office space at Rajapushpa West Avenue in Hyderabad's Kokapet through five lease agreements with Rajapushpa Realty LLP.
